---
title: "Simulation Study — ARMA(1,1)"
author: "Akshaan Murugesu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      fig.height = 4, fig.width = 6)
```

```{r libraries}
library(parallel)
library(pracma)
ncores <- max(1, detectCores() - 1)
```

```{r source}
source(here::here("R", "01_core_estimators.R"))
source(here::here("R", "02_dgm.R"))
source(here::here("R", "03_drivers.R"))
source(here::here("R", "04_summary_tables.R"))
```

## Simulation design

```{r params}
set.seed(2025)

nsim        <- 1000
phi_true    <- 0.2
theta_true  <- 0.4
n_values    <- c(50, 75, 100, 200, 300)
alpha_grid  <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)
innovations <- c("gaussian", "t5", "chisq5", "exp1")
rds_file    <- here::here("outputs", "results_arma11.rds")
```

```{r run-or-load}
if (file.exists(rds_file)) {
  results_arma11 <- readRDS(rds_file)
  message("Loaded cached results from ", rds_file)
} else {
  results_arma11 <- setNames(vector("list", length(innovations)), innovations)
  for (innov in innovations) {
    results_arma11[[innov]] <- list()
    for (n in n_values) {
      cat("ARMA(1,1) |", innov, "| n =", n, "\n")
      results_arma11[[innov]][[as.character(n)]] <- run_hybrid_grid_arma11(
        n = n, nsim = nsim,
        phi_true = phi_true, theta_true = theta_true,
        alpha_grid = alpha_grid, innov = innov, ncores = ncores
      )
    }
  }
  saveRDS(results_arma11, rds_file)
  message("Results saved to ", rds_file)
}
```

## Plotting helpers

```{r helpers}
pretty_innov_expr <- function(innov) {
  switch(innov,
    gaussian = quote(N(0,1)), t5 = quote(t[5]),
    chisq5   = quote(chi^2[5]), exp1 = quote(Exp(1)), as.name(innov))
}
add_headroom <- function(ylim, pct = 0.05) {
  ylim[2] <- ylim[2] + pct * diff(ylim); ylim
}
add_yaxis_5dec <- function() {
  yt <- axTicks(2); axis(2, at = yt, labels = sprintf("%.5f", yt))
}
```

## Plots: summed RMSE, functional boxplots, boxplots

```{r plots}
col_curve   <- "blue"
col_whittle <- "purple"
col_fdel    <- "darkgreen"

for (innov in names(results_arma11)) {
  innov_lab <- pretty_innov_expr(innov)
  for (n in n_values) {
    res        <- results_arma11[[innov]][[as.character(n)]]
    phi_wh     <- res$phi_wh;    theta_wh  <- res$theta_wh
    phi_fd     <- res$phi_fd;    theta_fd  <- res$theta_fd
    phi_hyb    <- res$phi_hyb;   theta_hyb <- res$theta_hyb
    alpha_grid_l <- res$alpha_grid

    rmse_sum_alpha <- apply(phi_hyb, 2, rmse, true = phi_true) +
                      apply(theta_hyb, 2, rmse, true = theta_true)
    interior   <- alpha_grid_l > 0 & alpha_grid_l < 1
    idx_best   <- which(interior)[which.min(rmse_sum_alpha[interior])]
    alpha_best <- alpha_grid_l[idx_best]
    phi_best   <- phi_hyb[,   idx_best]
    th_best    <- theta_hyb[, idx_best]

    ## ---- Summed RMSE curve -------------------------------------------------
    plot(alpha_grid_l, rmse_sum_alpha, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = col_curve,
         xlab = expression(alpha),
         ylab = expression(RMSE[phi] + RMSE[theta]),
         ylim = add_headroom(range(rmse_sum_alpha, na.rm = TRUE)),
         yaxt = "n",
         main = bquote(atop(
           "Summed RMSE(" * hat(phi) + "," * hat(theta) * ") for ARMA(1,1)",
           T == .(n) ~ ", innovations" ~ .(innov_lab))))
    grid(col = "grey90", lty = "dotted")
    add_yaxis_5dec()
    abline(v = alpha_best, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
    text(alpha_best, min(rmse_sum_alpha),
         bquote(alpha["best"] == .(round(alpha_best, 2))), pos = 4, cex = 0.7)
    points(0, rmse_sum_alpha[alpha_grid_l == 0], pch = 19, col = col_fdel)
    points(1, rmse_sum_alpha[alpha_grid_l == 1], pch = 19, col = col_whittle)
    text(0, rmse_sum_alpha[alpha_grid_l == 0], "FDEL",    pos = 3, cex = 0.7, xpd = NA)
    text(1, rmse_sum_alpha[alpha_grid_l == 1], "Whittle", pos = 3, cex = 0.7, xpd = NA)

    ## ---- Functional boxplots for phi and theta -----------------------------
    for (param_info in list(
      list(mat = phi_hyb,   true_val = phi_true,   ylab = expression(hat(phi)),
           label = "phi"),
      list(mat = theta_hyb, true_val = theta_true, ylab = expression(hat(theta)),
           label = "theta")
    )) {
      sq   <- apply(param_info$mat, 2, quantile, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
      q25  <- sq[1, ]; q50 <- sq[2, ]; q75 <- sq[3, ]
      ylim <- range(q25, q75, param_info$true_val, na.rm = TRUE)

      plot(alpha_grid_l, q50, type = "n", ylim = ylim,
           xlim = c(min(alpha_grid_l) - 0.02, max(alpha_grid_l) + 0.02),
           xlab = expression(alpha), ylab = param_info$ylab,
           main = bquote(atop(
             "Hybrid" ~ .(as.name(param_info$label)) ~ "across" ~ alpha ~ "for ARMA(1,1)",
             T == .(n) ~ ", innovations" ~ .(innov_lab))))
      grid(col = "grey90", lty = "dotted")
      polygon(c(alpha_grid_l, rev(alpha_grid_l)), c(q25, rev(q75)),
              col = adjustcolor("lightblue", 0.5), border = NA)
      lines(alpha_grid_l, q25, col = col_curve, lwd = 1.5, lty = 2)
      lines(alpha_grid_l, q75, col = col_curve, lwd = 1.5, lty = 2)
      lines(alpha_grid_l, q50, col = col_curve, lwd = 2)
      abline(h = param_info$true_val, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
      legend("topleft",
             legend = c("Median", "25–75% band",
                        if (param_info$label == "phi") expression(phi[true])
                        else expression(theta[true])),
             lty = c(1, NA, 2), lwd = c(2, NA, 2),
             pch = c(NA, 15, NA), pt.cex = c(NA, 2, NA),
             col = c(col_curve, adjustcolor("lightblue", 0.5), "red"), bty = "n")
    }

    ## ---- Boxplots for phi and theta ----------------------------------------
    lab_expr <- eval(bquote(expression(
      Whittle, FDEL, Hybrid ~ alpha == .(round(alpha_best, 2)))))

    for (bp_info in list(
      list(wh = phi_wh, fd = phi_fd, best = phi_best,
           true_val = phi_true, ylab = expression(hat(phi)),
           label = "phi"),
      list(wh = theta_wh, fd = theta_fd, best = th_best,
           true_val = theta_true, ylab = expression(hat(theta)),
           label = "theta")
    )) {
      boxplot(list(Whittle = bp_info$wh, FDEL = bp_info$fd, Hybrid = bp_info$best),
              main = bquote(atop(
                "Estimators of" ~ .(as.name(bp_info$label)) ~ "for ARMA(1,1)",
                T == .(n) ~ ", innovations" ~ .(innov_lab) ~
                  "," ~ alpha["best"] == .(round(alpha_best, 2)))),
              ylab = bp_info$ylab,
              col  = c(col_whittle, col_fdel, col_curve),
              names = rep("", 3), xaxt = "n")
      abline(h = bp_info$true_val, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
      axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = lab_expr, tick = FALSE)
    }

    ## ---- Summed RMSE barplot -----------------------------------------------
    rmse_sum_vec <- c(
      Whittle = rmse(phi_wh, phi_true) + rmse(theta_wh, theta_true),
      FDEL    = rmse(phi_fd, phi_true) + rmse(theta_fd, theta_true),
      Hybrid  = rmse(phi_best, phi_true) + rmse(th_best, theta_true)
    )
    bp <- barplot(rmse_sum_vec,
                  main = bquote(atop(
                    "Summed RMSE for ARMA(1,1)",
                    T == .(n) ~ ", innovations" ~ .(innov_lab) ~
                      "," ~ alpha["best"] == .(round(alpha_best, 2)))),
                  ylab = expression(RMSE[phi] + RMSE[theta]),
                  ylim = c(0, max(rmse_sum_vec) * 1.25),
                  col  = c(col_whittle, col_fdel, col_curve),
                  names.arg = rep("", 3), xaxt = "n")
    text(bp, rmse_sum_vec, labels = round(rmse_sum_vec, 4), pos = 3, cex = 0.8)
    axis(1, at = bp, labels = lab_expr, tick = FALSE)

  }
}
```

## Summary tables

```{r table}
big_table_arma11 <- make_big_table_arma11(results_arma11)
print(big_table_arma11)
```

```{r latex, results='asis'}
to_latex_table_arma(
  big_table_arma11,
  caption = paste0("Bias, SD and RMSE for $\\phi$ and $\\theta$ and summed RMSE of",
                   " Whittle, FDEL and Hybrid estimators for ARMA(1,1) with",
                   " $(\\phi_0,\\theta_0)=(0.2,0.4)$ under different innovations.")
)
```
